meta.value: diagrams (for the rendered blocks it emits)

blocks.heading: The layer that reads and *understands* a subject repo — schemas, routers, imports — so the products can ground their output in real code.

rows.value: The parsed-code model: import edges and the module dependency graph, parsed Prisma models, tRPC procedures, and the diffs between two API or schema surfaces.

Pure analysis. It takes source text (a `schema.prisma`, a router `.ts`, any TS/JS file) and a git scope, and returns structured facts — never opinions, never narrative. The TypeScript compiler is its parser of choice.
Without it, every product would emit a generic file-tree and a raw diff. With it, a Prisma + tRPC repo gets schema- and router-level understanding a generic repo can't.

bigIdea.label: The load-bearing idea
bigIdea.sub: `selectAdapter` picks the first adapter whose `detect()` passes (a `prisma/schema.prisma` exists → **PrismaTrpcAdapter**), else falls back to **GenericAdapter** — which returns *nothing*. So a Prisma + tRPC repo gets schema- and router-level understanding that any other repo silently does without, with no special-casing in the products themselves.

blocks.title: The pieces
blocks.intro: Two layers: thin **stack adapters** that detect a repo's stack and orchestrate, and the **parsers** they (and the atlas) call to turn source text into structured facts. Tap a card to jump to its full section.

cards.name: stack adapters
cards.purpose: The stack-aware front door. `StackAdapter` is the interface (detect / schemaDiff / apiDiff); `PrismaTrpcAdapter` wires the parsers together for a Prisma+tRPC repo; `GenericAdapter` is the no-op fallback. `selectAdapter` chooses.

cards.name: prisma-schema
cards.purpose: Parses a `schema.prisma` into models (name + typed fields), diffs two parsings into added / removed / kept fields, and renders the changed models as a D2 ERD.

cards.name: trpc-parse
cards.purpose: Walks a tRPC router source with the TS compiler: finds the `router({ ... })` object and reads each procedure's auth (`publicProcedure` → `public`), kind (query / mutation), and `.input(...)` shape.

cards.name: imports
cards.purpose: Extracts import / export-from / dynamic-import specifiers from a source file (with a `valueOnly` mode that drops type-only edges), plus the exported binding names of a module.

cards.name: dep-graph
cards.purpose: Resolves import specifiers through tsconfig path aliases and assembles a bounded 1-hop import neighborhood around a set of changed files — the mechanical `where-it-fits` diagram.

cards.name: api-diff
cards.purpose: Diffs two `ApiProcedure[]` surfaces by name and signature (auth · kind · input) into an `ApiBlock` containing only the added / changed / removed procedures.

blocks.title: How a subject repo gets understood
blocks.intro: `selectAdapter` probes the repo. If `prisma/schema.prisma` exists, the **PrismaTrpcAdapter** feeds that schema into `parsePrismaModels` and each changed `src/server/routers/*.ts` into `parseRouter`, then diffs both. Independently, the atlas gather walks every source file through `importsOf` and hands it to `dependencyNeighborhood`. Everything bare-package is dropped — only in-repo edges survive.

diagram.mermaid:
graph LR
  schema["prisma/schema.prisma"]
  routers["src/server/routers/*.ts"]
  srcfiles["any *.ts / *.tsx"]
  select["selectAdapter detect → adapter"]
  adapter["PrismaTrpcAdapter"]
  prisma["parsePrismaModels + diffModels"]
  trpc["parseRouter + diffProcedures"]
  imports["importsOf"]
  dep["dependencyNeighborhood"]
  models["PrismaModel typed fields"]
  select -->|picks| adapter
  schema -->|schemaDiff| adapter
  routers -->|apiDiff| adapter
  adapter --> prisma
  adapter --> trpc
  prisma --> models
  srcfiles --> imports
  imports -->|resolveModule| dep
  classDef external fill:#f1f3f5,stroke:#adb5bd,color:#1b1b1b;
  classDef actor fill:#d0ebff,stroke:#4dabf7,color:#1b1b1b;
  classDef store fill:#e5dbff,stroke:#9775fa,color:#1b1b1b;
  class schema,routers,srcfiles external;
  class select actor;
  class models store;
diagram.caption: The subject repo's files (gray) are *external input* — code-intel never writes them, only reads them.

blocks.title: The pieces, in depth
blocks.intro: A full section per piece — what it does, how it works, its files and exports, and how it connects to the rest of the toolkit.

components.name: stack adapters
detail: `StackAdapter` is a four-method interface: `name`, `detect(repoRoot)`, `schemaDiff(scope)`, and `apiDiff(scope)`. `selectAdapter(repoRoot, adapters)` returns the first adapter whose `detect()` resolves true, falling back to a fresh `GenericAdapter` — so selection is just an ordered probe, and the fallback always wins last.

`PrismaTrpcAdapter` is the one concrete stack adapter. `detect` checks for `prisma/schema.prisma`. `schemaDiff` reads that file at the base and head refs (`fileAtRef`), parses both with [parsePrismaModels](#c-prisma-schema), diffs them, and returns a `SchemaBlock` — or `null` if nothing changed. `apiDiff` filters the changed files to `src/server/routers/*.ts` (excluding `_app.ts`), parses each with [parseRouter](#c-trpc-parse), and emits one `ApiBlock` per router via [diffProcedures](#c-api-diff). It is the seam where every other parser is wired together.

`GenericAdapter` is deliberately empty: `detect` always returns true, `schemaDiff` returns `null`, `apiDiff` returns `[]`. A non-Prisma repo therefore gets a clean file-tree-and-diff recap with no broken schema/API sections.

files.name: prisma-trpc.ts
files.desc: Detects `prisma/schema.prisma`; wires the schema + router parsers and diffs together.

files.name: generic.ts
files.desc: The no-op fallback: `detect` true, no schema, no API.

exports.name: selectAdapter()
exports.desc: → first adapter whose `detect()` passes, else a new `GenericAdapter`.

exports.name: PrismaTrpcAdapter · GenericAdapter
exports.desc: The concrete adapter and its fallback.

connections.body: `fileAtRef` / `changedFiles` from `git.ts` to get source at the base & head refs
[recap](../domain-recap/domain-recap.html) — `gather-recap` calls `selectAdapter(repoRoot, [new PrismaTrpcAdapter()])` for schema + API blocks

components.name: prisma-schema
detail: `parsePrismaModels(schema)` regex-matches `model X { ... }` blocks into a `Map<name, PrismaModel>`, where each field is `{ name, type }` taken from the first two whitespace-separated tokens of a line (skipping comments and `@@` block attributes). It is a pragmatic parser, not a full Prisma grammar — enough to drive a diff and an ERD.

`diffModels(before, after)` unions the model names and, for each, computes added / removed / kept fields by name; a model that appears only on one side is also flagged. `schemaDiffToBlock(diffs)` then renders the changed models as a single D2 `sql_table` ERD with `(+ added)` / `(removed)` markers and the `changed` class — the `SchemaBlock` the recap shows.

files.name: prisma-schema.ts
files.desc: Schema parse, field diff, and the D2-ERD `SchemaBlock` renderer.

exports.name: diffModels()
exports.desc: → `ModelDiff[]` — added / removed / kept fields per model.

exports.name: schemaDiffToBlock()
exports.desc: → `SchemaBlock` — changed models as a D2 ERD with change markers.

components.name: trpc-parse
detail: `parseRouter(source, routerName)` builds a TS `SourceFile` and finds the first `router({ ... })` call's object literal. Each property is one procedure; it walks the property's call chain backward — `.query` / `.mutation` / `.subscription` sets the `kind`, `.input(...)` captures the raw input expression text, and a trailing `xxxProcedure` identifier yields the `auth` (e.g. `publicProcedure` → `public`).

The result is an `ApiProcedure[]` of `{ name: "router.proc", auth, kind, input }`. It is purely syntactic — it reads the builder chain, not the runtime types — which is exactly enough to diff two surfaces.

files.name: trpc-parse.ts
files.desc: TS-AST walk of a `router({...})` object into per-procedure `{ auth, kind, input }`.

connections.body: [PrismaTrpcAdapter](#c-adapters) `apiDiff` — before & after for each changed router
[atlas](../domain-atlas/domain-atlas.html) — `gather-atlas` parses routers for the API inventory
[api-diff](#c-api-diff) — its output is the input to `diffProcedures`

components.name: imports
detail: `importsOf(source, { valueOnly })` collects module specifiers from `import`, `export … from`, and dynamic `import(...)` via the TS AST, deduped. With `valueOnly`, fully type-only declarations are skipped — they carry no runtime dependency, so they shouldn't count as architectural edges (a mixed `import { type T, value }` still counts).

`exportsOf(source)` returns a module's exported binding names — named decls, `export { ... }`, re-exports, and `export default` as `"default"`. Together they are the raw material for both the dependency graph and the atlas's per-module inventory.

exports.name: exportsOf()
exports.desc: → `string[]` — exported binding names of a module.

connections.body: [dep-graph](#c-dep-graph) — every specifier `importsOf` finds becomes a candidate edge

components.name: dep-graph
detail: `dependencyNeighborhood(changedPaths, repoRoot)` builds the bounded 1-hop import neighborhood around the changed source files: their outgoing imports and the in-repo files that import them. It calls [importsOf](#c-imports) per file and `resolveModule` to turn each specifier into a `moduleKey` — relative specifiers join + normalize; bare packages resolve to `null` and are dropped as noise. `loadAliases` reads tsconfig `paths`/`baseUrl` so aliased imports (`@/...`) resolve too (a diagram-aid heuristic, not full module resolution).

Nodes are capped (default 15) by keeping all changed files plus the highest-degree neighbors, with a `+N more` placeholder for the rest. It emits both a D2 floor and an editable mermaid `graph LR` — the recap's mechanical **`where-it-fits`** diagram. `walkSource` (the recursive source lister, skipping `node_modules`/`dist`/…) backs the importer scan.

files.name: dep-graph.ts
files.desc: Alias-aware module resolution, the 1-hop neighborhood, and dual D2 + mermaid output.

exports.name: dependencyNeighborhood()
exports.desc: → `DiagramBlock | null` — the `where-it-fits` neighborhood, or null if no edges.

exports.name: resolveModule()
exports.desc: Specifier (relative or tsconfig-alias) → in-repo `moduleKey`, else null.

exports.name: loadAliases() · walkSource() · moduleKey()
exports.desc: tsconfig paths, the recursive source lister, and the path-normalizer.

connections.body: [importsOf](#c-imports) per file to discover candidate edges
[recap](../domain-recap/domain-recap.html) — `gather-recap` calls `dependencyNeighborhood` on the changed file set
[atlas](../domain-atlas/domain-atlas.html) — `gather-atlas` builds its module graph from the same import edges

components.name: api-diff
detail: `diffProcedures(before, after, title, id)` is the pure surface diff. It keys each side by procedure name and compares a signature string of `auth | kind | input`: a name missing before is `added`, a name with a changed signature is `changed`, a name missing after is `removed`. The result is an `ApiBlock` carrying *only* the changed procedures — unchanged ones are dropped so the recap shows just the delta.
It is stack-agnostic — it diffs `ApiProcedure[]`, not tRPC source — so the same diff would serve any future adapter that can produce that shape.

files.name: api-diff.ts
files.desc: Name + signature (auth · kind · input) diff into an added/changed/removed `ApiBlock`.

exports.name: diffProcedures()
exports.desc: → `ApiBlock` — only the added / changed / removed procedures.

blocks.title: Data it owns
blocks.intro: The **parsed-code model** — the structured facts it extracts from a subject repo's source. It owns these shapes; the products consume them.

rows.name: import specifiers · the dependency graph
rows.desc: Per-file import / export edges from `importsOf`, resolved to in-repo module keys and assembled into the bounded `where-it-fits` neighborhood.

rows.name: PrismaModel · PrismaField · ModelDiff
rows.desc: Parsed Prisma models (name + typed fields) and the added / removed / kept field diff between two schema versions.

rows.name: ApiProcedure
rows.desc: A parsed tRPC procedure: `{ name, auth, kind, input }`, read syntactically from the router builder chain.

rows.name: Aliases
rows.desc: The tsconfig `paths` / `baseUrl` matchers used to resolve aliased import specifiers to module keys.

rows.name: the API & schema surface diffs
rows.desc: `ApiBlock` (added/changed/removed procedures) and `SchemaBlock` (changed-model ERD) — the rendered deltas the recap shows.

blocks.note: Reads (never owns): the subject repo's source files and `prisma/schema.prisma` — pure external input, accessed through `git.ts` (`fileAtRef`, `changedFiles`).

blocks.title: Seams
blocks.intro: Source in (via git refs); structured facts and rendered diagram blocks out. The products call these parsers; code-intel calls nothing back.
blocks.note: `dep-graph` and `prisma-schema` import from **diagrams** (`MERMAID_CLASSDEFS`, the `DiagramBlock` / `SchemaBlock` types) to emit colored, render-ready blocks. The TS compiler is the workhorse for `trpc-parse`, `imports`, and alias loading. No callbacks into the products — code-intel is a leaf the products pull from.
